title: tokenize collects entire source to Vec<char> before processing
description: lexer.rs:34: `let chars: Vec<char> = src.chars().collect()` allocates a heap copy of every char in the source before any tokenization. A Peekable<Chars> iterator would process the source in a single pass with no upfront allocation. The current approach doubles memory for the source. For a 100KB Sieve script this is 100KB extra (likely ~400KB in Vec<char> due to char = 4 bytes).
